steps.title: Identify what the pattern suggests
steps.content: The passage describes AACM's mentorship for young musicians, then states that numerous organizations offer similar kinds of support to young painters, writers, and other artists. The word “similar” is the key signal: it reveals that organizations across different art forms implement the same mentorship practice, suggesting they all recognize its value.

steps.title: Match the signal to the answer
steps.content:
Choice B identifies exactly what this pattern reveals: organizations recognize the importance of providing mentorship opportunities to young artists. This explains why we see similar programs across music, visual arts, and writing.

The other three choices either create unsupported comparisons (A compares training vs. guidance; D claims musicians benefit more), reverse the focus to benefit established artists (C), or contradict the “similar” pattern.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
